specification.topic-statement: The LLM request builder assembles a system prompt from pinned documents, selects messages from the active context window, applies an optional tool filter, and dispatches the request asynchronously to the configured provider.

scope.in-scope: Submission trigger, system prompt assembly from pinned documents, message selection using the context window index, tool definition assembly with whitelist filtering, thinking level configuration, asynchronous dispatch, and maximum output token handling.
scope.boundary: How the assembled request is serialized into a provider-specific JSON body (Anthropic Messages API, OpenAI Chat Completions, Google Generative Language) is handled by the respective provider implementations and is out of scope.

How the context window index is advanced when the token budget is exceeded is covered by the token cache specification. This specification covers only how that index is consumed when selecting messages.
The token cache tracks per-turn token costs and advances a context start index when cumulative token usage exceeds the budget. Messages before the context start index are excluded from subsequent requests.
What happens after the request is dispatched — streaming response parsing, tool call extraction, and state transitions — is covered by the SSE parser and agent state machine specifications.

field.name: system prompt
field.note: assembled from pinned documents; null if no system prompt is available

field.name: messages
field.note: conversation messages from the context start index to the end of the in-memory array; deep-copied from agent state

field.name: thinking
field.note: the agent's configured thinking level; determines whether thinking blocks are requested and at what budget

field.name: tools
field.note: tools from the registry, filtered by the agent's toolset whitelist if one is set

field.name: max output tokens
field.note: defaults to absent (provider chooses); set to a provider-specific default when thinking is enabled

behavior.trigger: An LLM request is submitted when the user presses Enter and the input buffer does not begin with `/` (which would indicate a slash command). The current text is extracted from the input buffer, the buffer is cleared, and the text is submitted as a new user message.
behavior.user-message-addition: Before the request is built, the submitted text is added to the agent's in-memory message array as a user-role text message. The token cache is notified of a new turn boundary. The message is also persisted asynchronously to the database; a database write failure is logged but does not prevent the request from proceeding.
behavior.system-prompt-assembly:
The system prompt is assembled using the first available source in the following priority order:
1. **Pinned documents**: if the agent has one or more pinned document paths, their contents are read from the document cache in the order they were pinned and concatenated to form the system prompt. 2. **Default system file**: if no pinned documents are set, the application looks for a default system prompt file in the data directory. If the file exists, its content is used. 3. **Configuration fallback**: if neither of the above is available, the system prompt from the application configuration is used.
If none of the three sources yields a non-empty string, the request is sent without a system prompt.
behavior.message-selection:
Messages are selected from the agent's in-memory message array starting at the context start index provided by the token cache. All messages from that index to the end of the array are included. Each selected message is deep- copied into the request; the agent's in-memory array is not modified.
If no token cache is configured, the context start index is zero and all messages are included.
Messages before the context start index (those that have been pruned from the active context window) are excluded. They remain in the agent's in-memory array but are not sent to the provider.
behavior.tool-assembly:
Tool definitions are added to the request from the shared tool registry. If the agent has a toolset whitelist (a non-empty list of allowed tool names), only tools whose names appear in the whitelist are added. If no whitelist is set, all registered tools are added.
For each included tool, the name, description, and parameter schema are extracted from the registry entry's stored schema document.
behavior.thinking-configuration:
The agent's configured thinking level is set on the request. Provider serializers interpret this level to include provider-specific thinking parameters in the API request body. When thinking is enabled on a budget- based model, the maximum output tokens is adjusted upward to accommodate the thinking budget in addition to the response output.
When thinking is at its minimum level (disabled), no thinking configuration is included in the serialized request.
behavior.async-dispatch:
The assembled request is dispatched by calling the provider's start-stream function, which registers the HTTP request with the application's multiplexed HTTP session manager and returns immediately. The request is processed asynchronously on subsequent event loop iterations as data becomes available.
The agent's provider instance is created lazily on the first request if it has not yet been initialized. If the provider cannot be initialized (for example, because the API key is absent), the request is not submitted and an error is displayed.
The request object is allocated on a temporary context and freed after the start-stream call returns. The provider retains only what it needs internally for the in-flight HTTP session; it does not hold a reference to the request structure.
